comment: Scene manifest for scripts/screenshots.mjs. These screenshots serve two jobs at once: they are embedded in the product docs (README + docs/guide/), and they are the visual-regression baselines CI compares against. Only scenes worth SHOWING A USER belong here — if a scene isn't worth documenting, it isn't worth a golden. Every image under docs/screenshots/ must come from this manifest. Two kinds: still scenes (default) capture one PNG and are pixel-compared in CI; 'kind': 'recording' scenes script an interaction ('actions') and render an animated GIF — step-frames, one per action — which is DOCUMENTATION ONLY: recordings are never pixel-compared (animation diffs are flake bait; a flaky check teaches agents to regenerate goldens blindly), so they regenerate only via --update and changes are pm-reviewed like any baseline.

scene_policy.comment: When does a visual change warrant a NEW scene? These criteria are the architect's judgment aid — the architect cites the matching one in the spec's `visual:` declaration, and the adversary can challenge that declaration at the spec gate. That challenge is the enforcement; this block is guidance, not a machine-checked policy. An EXISTING baseline whose pixels change is always regenerated and pm-reviewed regardless.
new_scene_when: a new user-visible state a user must recognize (a new empty/full/error/loading state, a new mode)
a new interactive affordance a user must find (a new control, button, input, or gesture)
a new page or task area in the user guide that has no illustrating image
no_new_scene_when: refinement of an already-documented state: spacing, color, typography, copy edits, element reordering (existing baselines that change still get regenerated + reviewed)
the change is visible only transiently or is not meaningfully capturable (announce-only, focus behavior) — declare `visual: none — <why>`

scenes.description: First run — empty list, ready for a first todo
Adding two todos and completing one — the core interaction loop
A working list: three items, one completed, remaining counter live
List at the 10-item cap — adding is blocked with an explanation
Just after deleting a todo — a 5-second Undo affordance appears inline
Due dates — relative time-left labels, a highlighted overdue row, and the sort-by-date toggle
Editing a todo — the text field, the date field, and the explicit Save/Cancel buttons
Reordering a todo — focusing the drag handle reveals it and the keyboard hint, then ArrowDown moves the item one position with a live announcement
